equipment.description: Gypsum Rock Crusher; American Pulverizer Company, model AC-3B-BG;
Gypsum Rock Crusher; American Pulverizer Company, model AC-3B-BG;

background.text:
Background Draft Engineering Evaluation
Facility #173; Application #31097 
Georgia-Pacific Gypsum LLC 
801 Minaker Drive, Antioch, CA 94509 
Georgia-Pacific Gypsum LLC (GP) submitted this application for Change of Conditions for the 
following equipment at its facility (BAAQMD Facility# 173) in Antioch, CA: 
S-93 Gypsum Rock Crusher; American Pulverizer Company, model AC-3B-BG; 
Back up Crusher; Maximum Rated Throughput: 125 tons/hour 
Abated by: A-3 Baghouse, Reverse Jet, 5000 dscfm 
S-95 Gypsum Rock Crusher; American Pulverizer Company, model AC-3B-BG; 
Primary Crusher; Maximum Rated Throughput: 125 tons/hour 
Abated by: A-3 Baghouse, Reverse Jet, 5000 dscfm 
This plant produces agricultural gypsum, plaster and wallboard products. The facility receives 
gypsum rock ore via ships and stores it in a dome located on the property. Rock from the dome 
is fed into a crusher (S-93 or S-95), and it reduces in size from 4" to less than%". The crushed 
gypsum ore is conveyed to the main processing building, where it is further reduced in size using 
roller mills and then calcined to stucco. 
Gypsum Rock Crushers S-93 and S-95 are identical equipment, and they will be interchanged 
into the process when one undergoes repairs. The owner/operator shall not operate the two 
sources concurrently per operating permit condition #26164.2. 
This application is for change in operating condition #26164 for S-93 and S-95. The current 
conditions require the owner/operator to conduct annual testing and submit reports to show the

emission_calculations.text:
Emissions Summary
Both crushers are powered by electricity and there will be no combustion-related emissions. 
Particulate Matter (PM) emission will be generated during the crushing process.

common_fugitives.narrative:
Emissions Summary
Both crushers are powered by electricity and there will be no combustion-related emissions. 
Particulate Matter (PM) emission will be generated during the crushing process.

S-93.narrative: No equipment-specific narrative found in EMISSIONS section.

S-95.narrative: No equipment-specific narrative found in EMISSIONS section.

toxic_risk_screening_analysis.narrative:
toxic air contaminants (TAC) emissions are below the trigger levels in Regulation 2-5, Table 2-
5-1. The applicant is requesting to reduce testing frequency to once every three years.
The facility provided six previous lab test results that have shown emissions are well below the 
Regulation 2-5 limits. The facility is willing to accept more stringent TAC concentration limits 
to demonstrate compliance to justify the reduced testing frequency from annually to every three 
years.

blocks.heading: toxic air contaminants (TAC) emissions are below the trigger levels in Regulation 2-5, Table 2-
blocks.text:
toxic air contaminants (TAC) emissions are below the trigger levels in Regulation 2-5, Table 2-
5-1. The applicant is requesting to reduce testing frequency to once every three years.
The facility provided six previous lab test results that have shown emissions are well below the 
Regulation 2-5 limits. The facility is willing to accept more stringent TAC concentration limits 
to demonstrate compliance to justify the reduced testing frequency from annually to every three 
years.
